channel.title: briefUpdatesBreakingOffshootNL
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from briefUpdatesBreakingOffshootNL, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: The Behind the Scenes Search for Compromise on Territory in Ukraine Talks
item.description: The latest round of talks ended with no indication of progress, but negotiators are bargaining over who will control land in eastern Ukraine if they r...

item.title: Russia’s Exile From World Sports Will End Next Month at Paralympics
item.description: Six Russian athletes and four Belarusians will be allowed to represent their nations, officials said. The decision could pave the way for a Russian te...

item.title: The Justice Department is not acting like it used to, criminal defense lawyers note
item.description: Criminal defense lawyers are tracking when the Justice Department appears to rely on irregular charging practices, including aggressive legal theories...

item.title: Mark Zuckerberg faces a jury today. And, the top DHS spokesperson resigns her post
item.description: Mark Zuckerberg takes the stand today in a trial over whether social media companies are fueling the teen mental health crisis. And, Tricia McLaughlin...

item.title: Talks on Russia-Ukraine War Enter 2nd Day in Geneva
item.description: The discussions in Geneva were expected to focus on territorial issues, a major sticking point, but the short duration suggests major progress was not...

item.title: Ogden, Schumacher grab silver for U.S. in Olympic cross-country team sprint, Diggins falls short
item.description: U.S. cross-country skiers Ben Ogden and Gus Schumacher power to a silver medal in the men's team sprint. U.S. women led by Jessie Diggins finish off t...

item.title: Sweet redemption for Mikaela Shiffrin, who wins Olympic gold
item.description: Third race is the charm for Shiffrin, who won gold today after failing to podium in her first two races of the 2026 Olympic Games.

item.title: Greetings from Cairo, where lights and decorations transform the city during Ramadan
item.description: As Ramadan begins, traditional lanterns called fawanees brighten Cairo. They have become a symbol of Ramadan and are an almost-mandatory home decorati...

item.title: Russia’s Exile From World Sports Will End Next Month at Paralympics
item.description: Six Russian athletes and four Belarusians will be allowed to represent their nations, officials said. The decision could pave the way for a Russian te...

item.title: Billy Preston’s Music Was ‘Pure Joy.’ But His Life Ended in Tragedy.
item.description: The Beatles’ beloved collaborator died at 59 in 2006. A new documentary, “That’s the Way God Planned It,” explores what he long kept hidden.

item.title: The Behind the Scenes Search for Compromise on Territory in Ukraine Talks
item.description: The latest round of talks ended with no indication of progress, but negotiators are bargaining over who will control land in eastern Ukraine if they r...

item.title: Anna Akhmatova, Leading Soviet Poet, Is Dead
item.description: She was a towering figure in Soviet literature who was once silenced in a Stalinist literary purge.

item.title: Mikaela Shiffrin exorcises ghost of Olympic past.
item.description: After failing to medal in 2022, Shiffrin rebounded to win gold in slalom.

item.title: Talks on Russia-Ukraine War Enter 2nd Day in Geneva
item.description: The discussions in Geneva were expected to focus on territorial issues, a major sticking point, but the short duration suggests major progress was not...

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.description: The avalanche struck a guided backcountry skiing group near Truckee, Calif., near the end of a multiday trip, officials said. Six skiers have been res...
